source: Compiled from DOL ApprenticeshipUSA, BLS, trade union data

overview.note: Apprentices earn $60K-$80K during training vs. accumulating $30K-$100K+ in student debt

highDemandTrades.unionVsNonUnion: Union: Higher pay, benefits, pension. IBEW (International Brotherhood of Electrical Workers). Non-union: More flexibility, varied pay.
highDemandTrades.demandDrivers: EV infrastructure, solar installations, data center construction, building electrification

highDemandTrades.unionVsNonUnion: UA (United Association) is primary union. Strong benefits and pension.
highDemandTrades.demandDrivers: Aging infrastructure, green building codes, water conservation systems

highDemandTrades.unionVsNonUnion: HVAC unions (Sheet Metal Workers Local 66 and others) offer strong benefits and pension. Non-union work more flexible and accessible.
highDemandTrades.demandDrivers: Heat pump adoption, refrigerant transitions, smart building systems

highDemandTrades.unionVsNonUnion: Boilermakers and other welding unions offer strong apprenticeships. Non-union positions more readily available.
highDemandTrades.note: Underwater welding and pipeline welding can pay $100K-$200K but with significant hazard premium

highDemandTrades.unionVsNonUnion: IUEC (International Union of Elevator Constructors) controls nearly all apprenticeships and positions. Union-only pathway.
highDemandTrades.note: One of the highest-paid trades. IUEC union controls most apprenticeships. Very competitive to enter.

highDemandTrades.unionVsNonUnion: Ironworkers International union (Local 40, etc.) provides training and strong benefits. Non-union positions available through general contractors.
highDemandTrades.demandDrivers: Infrastructure spending, bridge repairs, high-rise construction

unionApprenticeship.howToApply: Contact local union hall. Most have annual application windows (often spring). Aptitude test + interview. Some require references.
unionApprenticeship.bestFor: Those wanting maximum structure, benefits, and long-term career stability

nonUnionApprenticeship.howToApply: Apply directly to contractors or through trade schools. ABC (Associated Builders and Contractors) runs many programs.
nonUnionApprenticeship.bestFor: Those wanting faster entry or planning to start own business

tradeSchool.bestFor: Career changers wanting to test a trade before committing to apprenticeship

stateByStateLicensing.note: Licensing requirements vary significantly by state. Always check your state's requirements.

generalPattern.electrician: Most states require journeyman license after completing apprenticeship + passing exam
generalPattern.plumber: Most states require journeyman license. Master plumber license needed to run own business.
generalPattern.hvac: EPA 608 certification required nationally. State requirements vary.
generalPattern.welder: AWS certification widely accepted. No state license typically required but certifications improve pay.

militaryToTrades.advantage: Military experience may count toward apprenticeship hours. GI Bill can supplement apprenticeship wages.
